message: Node started: Identify the mapping between final nodes and initial node sets. For each node in the final tree, determine which initial nodes it represents. The transformation is achieved by performing merges on siblings in the initial tree that are not siblings in the final tree. The sequence of operations must follow a bottom-up traversal of the initial tree to ensure that when two siblings are merged, their children have already been processed or are part of the union. Specifically, for every parent in the final tree, identify its children. Map these children back to the sets of nodes in the initial tree. For each set of initial nodes that should be represented by a single child in the final tree, perform pairwise merges of the initial nodes within that set, always picking the larger value as the survivor. The traversal order must be post-order to maintain structural integrity.
